ab: makiteng ku ziya mu niyam ruma’ mu kazu i Katratripulr, temakesi ku mu mupasalr mi lra mukuwa i Taihok kazuwa.
ch: 小時候我們家住在知本，上學以後，就搬到了台北，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Katratripulr. After I started school, we moved to Taipei and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: temakesi ku lra i tasyuē mu, temakesi ku parasan zatu kakuwayanan na Yēncumin, maw na mazalram ku lra kana Taiwan Yēncumin ulra marekamelrimelri tu kakuwayanan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: maw izu na kanini na ’ami na katalaulepan na derun mu, salrangan ku za i valri na ma’inay muruma’ i zekalr kiumaumalr kana dawdawan, maranger ku mazalram kanta mawan nanata kakuwayanan.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: ina dawadawan makawalun lra tu ’ami, maw mu nu mena’u ta mu kamawan za makanemen ziya tu ’ami.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: ku kiumalray intaw za kararuwa u marengay za papidaya za piningaiyan, nu marengay kana ngai na Pinuyumayan mu salaw dalus, sayhu marengay kana tinarpaayrang, ngai na Amis zi maw na ngai na Dipung.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的卑南語說得很流利，也會說閩南語、阿美話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Pinuyumayan, as well as Hokkien, Pangcah and Japanese.

ab: kiumalr ku kanintaw mu mazalram ku lra za intaw mu kinamakitengan menadanadam lra kana tinardadipung na ngai, i turuma’antaw mu maw na Amis zi, kanatu kiniyakarunan mu kazuwan na Payrang tu sinadam, maw izu na sayhu marengay za sakuwaniyani a piningaiyan.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是阿美族人，而他工作上認識許多閩南人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Pangcah, and he knows many Hokkien at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: a azu a kiyaumaumalr mi mu, izu na dawdawan mu marengay lra za sazu kanini na watralunan na kinawatranan nata kazaalraman.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: kara ’azi mi maruwa mazalram kinger peniya kana tu ngai, maw mu mi sasingaw pasirep kana inadaada na dingwa, karuwa ta ziya pakumawmaw kinger.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: lralrak ku ziya mu ’azi ku mazalram kananta mawan tu kakuwayanan, tremepa ku kaninku maira ku zaku ikayēncumin.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: kana makasazu lra ku kinazalram mu, maw na mazalram ku lra maw ku na Pinuyumayan, maw na kirekamelri na mawan, aruwa ta puitraitras kaninta na Pinuyumayan.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為卑南族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Pinuyumayan is unique and something to be proud of.
